meta.domain: ClaimEdge LLC — Public Adjusting & Insurance Claims
meta.editorial_policy: Canonical terms are law. Aliases listed under 'avoid' are prohibited in all ClaimEdge AI OS outputs.

subdomains.name: Claim Intake & Triage
subdomains.description: Initial client contact, information gathering, and claim viability assessment

terms.definition: The policyholder who experienced the property damage and is entitled to coverage benefits
A formal request by the Insured to the Carrier for payment under a policy due to covered damage
The process of receiving and documenting initial information from a prospective Insured
The legally binding contract between ClaimEdge (the Public Adjuster) and the Insured establishing representation terms and compensation
Letter of Representation — formal notice sent to the Carrier identifying ClaimEdge as the Insured's representative
Assignment of Benefits — legal transfer of the Insured's policy rights to ClaimEdge for claim processing
Statute of Limitations — the legal deadline for filing a claim lawsuit, varies by state and policy type

subdomains.name: Policy & Coverage Review
subdomains.description: Policy interpretation, coverage analysis, and exclusion identification

terms.definition: The insurance contract between the Insured and Carrier that defines covered perils, limits, and exclusions
The scope of protection provided by the Policy for specific perils and damage types
A provision in the Policy that specifically denies coverage for certain perils, conditions, or property types
A formal modification or addition to the Policy that alters coverage terms
The maximum dollar amount the Carrier will pay for a covered loss under the Policy
The amount the Insured must pay out-of-pocket before the Carrier's coverage obligation begins
Replacement Cost Value — the cost to repair or replace damaged property with materials of like kind and quality, without depreciation deduction
Actual Cash Value — the RCV minus applicable depreciation, representing the property's value at the time of loss
The reduction in property value due to age, wear, and obsolescence; can be recoverable (holdback) or non-recoverable
The depreciated amount the Carrier withholds pending proof that repairs were completed, after which it may be recoverable

subdomains.name: Inspections & Field Evidence
subdomains.description: On-site property inspection, damage documentation, and evidence collection

terms.definition: The on-site examination of damaged property to identify, document, and assess the scope of loss
Photographs, measurements, material samples, and observations collected during the Inspection
A numbered piece of Field Evidence attached to a formal claim submission or report
Date-stamped photographs of all damaged areas, showing both close-up detail and wide context
The photo documentation platform used by ClaimEdge to organize, geotag, and share Inspection photos
The detailed description of what work is required to restore the property to pre-loss condition, independent of pricing
The state of the property immediately before the damaging event occurred
Physical harm or destruction to insured property caused by a covered peril

subdomains.name: Exterior Damage Assessment
subdomains.description: Specialized evaluation of roofing, siding, gutter, and exterior envelope damage

terms.definition: The complete roof assembly including shingles/tiles, underlayment, decking, flashing, and ventilation
The structural substrate/sheathing beneath the roof covering that provides the nailing surface; also called roof sheathing or roof deck
The outermost weather-resistant layer of the Roofing System, typically asphalt, wood, or composite
Thin metal strips installed at joints, valleys, and penetrations to direct water away from the building envelope
The internal angle formed where two roof planes meet, requiring specialized flashing and water management
Weather Resistant Barrier — the protective layer beneath exterior cladding (siding/stucco) that prevents moisture intrusion
The exterior cladding material (vinyl, fiber cement, wood, etc.) that protects the building envelope from weather
The complete barrier separating the interior conditioned space from the exterior environment, including roof, walls, windows, doors, and foundation
Impact damage to roofing, siding, or exterior surfaces caused by hailstones, characterized by bruising, cracking, or displacement
Damage caused by high winds including lifted shingles, torn flashing, displaced siding, or structural movement
Damage resulting from water intrusion through compromised roofing, siding, or flashing, including rot, mold, and staining

subdomains.name: Code & Compliance
subdomains.description: Building code compliance, permit requirements, and regulatory adherence

terms.definition: The requirement to bring repaired or replaced components up to current adopted building code standards, often triggered by Ordinance or Law coverage
Ordinance or Law coverage — the policy provision that pays for additional costs to comply with building codes during repairs
The legally adopted set of construction standards (IRC, IBC, IECC) governing design, materials, and methods for the jurisdiction
International Residential Code — the building code governing one- and two-family dwellings and townhouses
International Building Code — the building code governing commercial and multi-family construction
Official governmental authorization required before commencing construction, repair, or replacement work
The local governmental jurisdiction (city, village, or county) responsible for enforcing building codes and issuing permits

subdomains.name: Estimate & Supplement Support
subdomains.description: Xactimate estimating, supplement preparation, and scope expansion

terms.definition: The industry-standard insurance estimating software used to calculate repair and replacement costs
The priced document produced in Xactimate that lists all materials, labor, and overhead required to complete the Scope of Work
An individual component entry in the Estimate specifying a material, labor operation, or service with unit price and quantity
A demand for additional claim payment beyond the Carrier's initial Estimate, supported by documentation of omitted or under-scoped items
Overhead and Profit — the contractor markup (typically 10% + 10%) applied to trades requiring supervision and coordination
The total dollar amount of the claim as calculated through Estimate review, independent of the Scope of Work description
The geographically adjusted unit costs for materials and labor maintained by Xactware and used within Xactimate
A disagreement between the Carrier's adjuster and ClaimEdge regarding what work is necessary to restore the property
A formal sworn statement submitted to the Carrier documenting the nature and amount of the loss claimed
The maximum Coverage A amount in a homeowners policy for the primary residence structure

subdomains.name: Carrier Communications
subdomains.description: All correspondence, negotiation, and dispute resolution with insurance carriers

terms.definition: The insurance company that issued the Policy and is responsible for paying the Claim
An employee of the Carrier who investigates and evaluates claims on behalf of the insurance company
A third-party contractor hired by the Carrier to handle claims, not a direct employee
A licensed advocate who represents the Insured (not the Carrier) in preparing, filing, and negotiating insurance claims
A formal dispute resolution process where an impartial umpire decides the amount of loss when the Insured and Carrier disagree on valuation
The Carrier's unreasonable delay, denial, or underpayment of a valid claim without proper basis, potentially creating legal liability
A Carrier letter stating they are investigating the claim but reserving the right to deny coverage later based on policy terms
The Carrier's formal refusal to pay any portion of the claim, usually citing policy exclusions or lack of coverage
The Carrier's decision to pay some but not all claimed damages, often omitting code upgrades or supplemental items
A second on-site inspection requested by either party when the initial scope or valuation is disputed

subdomains.name: Document Assembly
subdomains.description: Report generation, exhibit packaging, and final claim document preparation

terms.definition: A formal deliverable produced by ClaimEdge for the Insured or Carrier, such as a supplement letter or inspection report
A document containing supporting evidence such as photos, measurements, or expert opinions
A document with legal significance such as a Fee Agreement, LOR, AOB, or Proof of Loss
Any document that supports the claim such as repair estimates, contractor bids, or code citations
The organized collection of all Exhibits numbered and cross-referenced to the claim submission
A structured photo report generated from CompanyCam with geotagged images organized by damage area
The formal written demand sent to the Carrier requesting additional payment with supporting arguments and documentation
The complete compiled set of all claim documents, evidence, and correspondence assembled for litigation or final settlement

subdomains.name: Jurisdiction Research Library
subdomains.description: Legal references, statutes, case law, and regulatory resources

terms.definition: A written law passed by a legislative body, such as 215 ILCS 5/155 (Illinois Insurance Code)
Legal precedents established by court decisions that interpret statutes and guide future rulings
The geographical area (state, county, or municipality) whose laws govern the claim and legal proceedings
Internal policy documents used by insurance companies to standardize claim handling procedures and settlement ranges

subdomains.name: AI OS Infrastructure
subdomains.description: The ClaimEdge AI Operating System itself — skills, roles, automation, and governance

terms.definition: A reusable AI capability module with defined triggers, functions, and output formats that activates automatically when needed
A functional domain grouping related Skills together under a common business capability (e.g., Claim Intake & Triage)
The governance structure of 22 specialized AI agent roles that manage the ClaimEdge AI OS
The standard workflow: Intake → Inspection → Evidence → Code → Scope → Supplement → Carrier → Follow-up
A mandatory signoff checkpoint where a human must review and approve AI-generated outputs before they are client or carrier-facing
The activation phrase or condition that causes a Skill to automatically engage when mentioned in conversation
The classification system that determines whether AI output requires auto-run, internal review, or human signoff based on risk level

subdomains.name: Sports Ventures
subdomains.description: Buddy Boy Sports, wrestling, and baseball operations (non-claims)

terms.definition: Robert Krapil's portfolio of youth sports initiatives including wrestling tracking and baseball team management, separate from claims operations
terms.ambiguity_note: Never conflate with claims operations. Sports Ventures operates under separate skills and databases.

subdomains.name: Productization & R&D
subdomains.description: Stripe billing, SaaS product development, and future commercialization

terms.definition: The process of converting ClaimEdge's internal AI tools into commercial SaaS products available for purchase via Stripe
The payment processing platform used for ClaimEdge product billing and subscription management

resolved_ambiguities.rationale: Insured is the legally precise term from insurance policy language. Client is acceptable only in Robert's direct verbal communication with the person, never in documents or AI outputs.
resolved_ambiguities.code_rule: Use 'Insured' in all SKILL.md files, prompts, documents, and databases. Flag any instance of 'client' or 'customer' for replacement.
exceptions: Robert's direct verbal/text messages to the insured person

resolved_ambiguities.canonical: Always qualified — Public Adjuster, Staff Adjuster, or Independent Adjuster. Bare 'adjuster' is banned.
resolved_ambiguities.rationale: The unqualified term 'adjuster' creates dangerous ambiguity since three distinct roles exist. Each has different loyalties and authorities.
resolved_ambiguities.code_rule: All SKILL.md files must use qualified adjuster terms. A linter should reject bare 'adjuster' in all outputs.

resolved_ambiguities.rationale: Case implies litigation. Matter is attorney jargon. Claim is the industry-standard term for an insurance claim from first notice of loss through resolution.
resolved_ambiguities.code_rule: Use 'Claim' universally. Replace 'case' and 'matter' with 'Claim' except in legal litigation contexts.
exceptions: Active litigation contexts where 'case' is legally appropriate

resolved_ambiguities.canonical: Three distinct concepts: Scope of Work (what work is needed), Estimate (the priced Xactimate document), Valuation (the total dollar amount)
resolved_ambiguities.rationale: These are commonly conflated but represent fundamentally different things. The Scope describes work, the Estimate prices it, and Valuation is the resulting dollar figure.
resolved_ambiguities.code_rule: Always distinguish in SKILL.md files. Scope of Work = description. Estimate = Xactimate document. Valuation = dollar amount.
exceptions: Casual conversation where context makes the meaning clear

resolved_ambiguities.canonical: Always qualified — Output Document, Evidence Document, Legal Document, or Supporting Document
resolved_ambiguities.rationale: The bare word 'document' is too vague in a domain where multiple distinct document types exist with different legal and operational significance.
resolved_ambiguities.code_rule: All SKILL.md files must use qualified document types. A linter should flag bare 'document' when context doesn't make the type obvious.
exceptions: Generic references where the specific type is irrelevant to the point being made

banned_terms.replacement: Public Adjuster / Staff Adjuster / Independent Adjuster
